message:
fix(ft-api): tensor_xlog1py propagates NaN from y when x == 0 [bd-duf7]

scipy.special.xlog1py and torch.special.xlog1py both propagate NaN
from y even when x == 0 (their mask is x == 0 && !isnan(y)).
FrankenTorch's mask was just (x == 0), so it returned 0 for
(0, NaN) — diverging from scipy and silently swallowing NaN
on the masked branch. Surfaced by
torch_xlog1py_scipy_subprocess_conformance during the b5of work.

Tighten the where mask:
    isnan_y = tensor_isnan(y)
    not_isnan_y = 1 - isnan_y
    mask = (x == 0) * not_isnan_y    # element-wise AND on f64 0/1
    out = where(mask, 0, x * log1p(y))

Now (0, NaN) → NaN ✓
    (0, -1)  → 0  (the y == -1 pole convention preserved)
    (0, finite / +inf) → 0 (other masked-branch behaviors preserved)

Adds xlog1py_propagates_nan_from_y_when_x_is_zero regression test
in ft-api covering all four masked-branch combinations. Re-enables
the (0.0, NaN) case in torch_xlog1py_scipy_subprocess_conformance
that was trimmed when this divergence was first observed.

ft-api lib tests: 1136 passing (+1 from 1135).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -16801,18 +16801,17 @@ print(json.dumps({"polygamma": out}))
             (1.0, 0.0),
             (5.0, 0.0),
             (-1.0, 0.0),
-            // The masked branch: x == 0 should yield 0 regardless of y,
-            // even when y == -1 (log1p(-1) = -inf) or y < -1 (NaN).
-            // NB scipy's mask is (x == 0 && !isnan(y)) so NaN
-            // propagates when y is NaN; FrankenTorch's mask is just
-            // (x == 0), which swallows NaN from y. Tracked under
-            // frankentorch-duf7; (0.0, NaN) is intentionally omitted
-            // from this harness until the mask is tightened.
+            // The masked branch: x == 0 should yield 0 when y is
+            // not NaN (even when y == -1 / log1p(-1) = -inf or y < -1
+            // where log1p is NaN of its own). When y itself is NaN,
+            // scipy and FrankenTorch (post-bd-duf7 fix) both
+            // propagate NaN.
             (0.0, 1.0),
             (0.0, -0.5),
             (0.0, -1.0),
             (0.0, -2.0),
             (0.0, f64::INFINITY),
+            (0.0, f64::NAN),
             // x ≠ 0 / y == -1: scipy returns -inf for x > 0, +inf for
             // x < 0 (sign of x times -inf). Test both.
             (1.0, -1.0),
